select_when: Capability is genuinely expandable through feedback, repetition, and deliberate improvement rather than blocked by an immutable constraint.
Setbacks should trigger learning loops instead of identity threat, ego defense, or retreat from challenge.
A team or learner needs a healthier response to error, criticism, and developmental discomfort so improvement can compound over time.
Leaders want to reward improvement quality, receptivity to feedback, and process discipline rather than ego protection.
avoid_when: Growth language is being used to deny real structural limits, weak strategy, role mismatch, or missing resources.
High effort is being treated as sufficient even though the work is still poorly diagnosed, poorly prioritized, or detached from what produces results.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on feedback receptivity, process focus, and adaptive improvement rather than on motivational slogans.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor culture-program sprawl because the raw source is already strongest on growth as a disciplined response to challenge, feedback, and iteration.
open_questions: The source reaches into organizational transformation, but the operational file stays tight on improvement loops that change behavior and judgment quality.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit that growth mindset is strongest when capability can expand through feedback and repetition, when setbacks should trigger learning loops, and when culture should reward improvement over ego protection.

avoid_when.note: The source explicitly warns against denying real constraints and against confusing effort alone with effective progress.

input_type.note: The raw source activates around expandable capability and developmental barriers; the compact input label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The source produces feedback-friendly iterative improvement loops; the output label compresses that into a short operational type.

reasoning_types.note: The model depends on reflective updating and behavior-to-outcome beliefs, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
